source_pr.title: *: add succ filed to slow log and fix shallow copy problem when parse slow log file.
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
* Add `Succ` filed name to slow log and `SLOW_QUERY` table.
* change `SLOW_QUERY`,`Query` column type from `varchar(4096)` to `longtext`.
* fix shallow copy problem caused by the long query(len(query) > 4094).

### What is changed and how it works?
* Add `copy` for `getOneLine` function.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change

Side effects

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

symptoms: re = tk.MustQuery("select query from information_schema.slow_query order by time desc limit 1") (infoschema/tables_test.go)
failure_signature: re = tk.MustQuery("select query from information_schema.slow_query order by time desc limit 1") (infoschema/tables_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because the slow-log parser performed shallow copies of buffered lines: when parsing long queries, the underlying buffer could be reused/overwritten, leading to corrupted SQL strings being observed later (and test assertions failing intermittently). This PR fixes the aliasing by explicitly copying the line bytes returned from `getOneLine`, preventing mutable backing-buffer reuse from leaking into parsed results.
fix_pattern: Copy slow-log line bytes when reading/parsing (avoid shallow-copy aliasing of mutable buffers) so parsed SQL strings remain stable.

evidence: evidence: re = tk.MustQuery("select query from information_schema.slow_query order by time desc limit 1") (infoschema/tables_test.go)
files: executor/adapter.go, infoschema/slow_log.go, infoschema/slow_log_test.go, infoschema/tables_test.go, sessionctx/variable/session.go, sessionctx/variable/session_test.go
